chunks.text:
0) Environment Setup
Goal: Set up the project directory and virtual environment to ensure a clean, isolated Python 3.10+ workspace. Micro-steps:
Check Python version:
Command: python3 --version
Where: Terminal, in your home directory (e.g., ~/ on Linux/macOS or C:\Users\YourName on Windows). What/Why: Verifies Python 3.10 or higher is installed. The ruling requires 3.10+ for stdlib compatibility (e.g., dataclasses, pathlib). If you see a version <3.10 (e.g., 3.9), you’ll need to install Python 3.10+ (next step). Expected Output: Python 3.10.x or higher (e.g., 3.11.0). Install Python 3.10+ if needed:
If version is <3.10:
Linux (Ubuntu):
Command: sudo apt update && sudo apt install python3.10 python3.10-venv
Where: Terminal. What/Why: Installs Python 3.10 and its venv module. Ubuntu may not have 3.10 by default. macOS:
Command: brew install python@3.10
Where: Terminal (install Homebrew first if needed: /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"). What/Why: Homebrew installs Python 3.10. macOS’s default Python may be older. Windows:
Command: Download and run the Python 3.10+ installer from https://www.python.org/downloads/. Where: Browser, then run the installer. What/Why: Ensures Python 3.10+ is available. Check “Add Python to PATH” during installation. Verify: Run python3 --version (Linux/macOS) or python --version (Windows) again. Create project directory:
Command: mkdir -p protoforge/src/protoforge/memory protoforge/storage/agents protoforge/storage/logs protoforge/storage/memory_stack
Where: Terminal, in your home directory. What/Why: Creates the project structure per the ruling. src/protoforge/ holds Python code; storage/ holds agent JSON, logs, and memory files.

-p ensures parent directories are created without errors. Create virtual environment:
Command: cd protoforge && python3 -m venv .venv
Where: Terminal, after mkdir. What/Why: Creates a .venv folder for an isolated Python environment to avoid conflicts with system packages. venv is a stdlib module for managing virtual environments. Activate virtual environment:
Linux/macOS:
Command: source .venv/bin/activate
Where: Terminal, inside protoforge/. What/Why: Activates the venv, ensuring python and pip commands use the isolated environment. You’ll see (.venv) in your prompt. Windows (PowerShell):
Command: .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
Where: Terminal, inside protoforge/. What/Why: Same as above, for Windows. Prompt will show (.venv). Verify Python in venv:
Command: python --version
Where: Terminal, with venv activated. What/Why: Confirms the venv uses Python 3.10+. Should match or exceed 3.10.0. Ready to move on? Confirm the venv is activated and python --version shows 3.10+. If not, let me know what you see, and I’ll help troubleshoot. 1) Create File Structure and Empty __init__.py Files
Goal: Set up the Python package structure to make protoforge a module and ensure imports work. Micro-steps:
Create empty __init__.py files:
Command: touch src/protoforge/__init__.py src/protoforge/memory/__init__.py
Where: Terminal, inside protoforge/. On Windows, use: echo. > src\protoforge\__init__.py and echo. > src\protoforge\memory\__init__.py. What/Why: Empty __init__.py files make src/protoforge/ and src/protoforge/memory/ Python packages, allowing imports like from protoforge.memory.echo import write_echo. Required for the ruling’s module structure. Ready to move on? Check that src/protoforge/__init__.py and src/protoforge/memory/__init__.py exist (empty files).

protoforge.memory.echo import write_echo. Required for the ruling’s module structure. Ready to move on? Check that src/protoforge/__init__.py and src/protoforge/memory/__init__.py exist (empty files). If you hit issues (e.g., touch not found on Windows), let me know. 2) Create README.md Goal: Document how to run the CLI and list commands, per the ruling and Grok’s updated guidance. Micro-steps: Create README.md: Command: Copy the following into protoforge/README.md: markdown # ProtoForge MVP (CLI) ## Run ```bash source .venv/bin/activate # Windows: .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1 python -V # Must show 3.10 or higher; install Python 3.10+ if not python -m src.main Commands spawn [--name NAME] list input --json '{...}' hibernate restore logs echo [--tail N] logs drift [--tail N] help exit Notes All actions append JSON lines to storage/logs/echo.log with hash chain and to storage/logs/drift.log when entropy > 0.4. χ(t) timer is 500ms, bumps entropy by +0.2 when exceeded, logged, doesn’t crash CLI. Agents soft-blocked by drift; restore clears block. Agents auto-restore on init from storage/agents/*.json. Per-agent state persists to storage/memory_stack/_memory.json. Test

sn’t crash CLI.
Agents soft-blocked by drift; restore <id> clears block.
Agents auto-restore on init from storage/agents/*.json.
Per-agent state persists to storage/memory_stack/<id>_memory.json.
Test

bash
python -m src.main
pf> spawn writer
# Copy agent_id, e.g., 123e4567-...
pf> input 123e4567 --json '{"prompt":"Summarize MVP rules"}'
pf> list
pf> logs echo --tail 10
pf> logs drift --tail 10
pf> hibernate 123e4567
pf> restore 123e4567
pf> input 123e4567 --json '{"prompt":"Continue"}'
# Test drift
pf> input 123e4567 --json '{"prompt":"a" * 10000}'
pf> input 123e4567 --json '{"prompt":"Blocked?"}'  # Shows DRIFT_BLOCKED
pf> restore 123e4567
pf> input 123e4567 --json '{"prompt":"Unblocked"}'  # Works
# Test χ(t) exceed: edit orchestrator.py route_input to add time.sleep(1), then:
pf> input 123e4567 --json '{"prompt":"Slow"}'
pf> logs drift --tail 5  # Shows chi_exceeded
pf> exit
Acceptance Criteria

Test χ(t) exceed: edit orchestrator.py route_input to add time.sleep(1), then:
pf> input 123e4567 --json '{"prompt":"Slow"}'
pf> logs drift --tail 5  # Shows chi_exceeded
pf> exit
Acceptance Criteria

✅ spawn, input, list, hibernate, restore work without exceptions. ✅ echo.log appends JSON lines with ts, actor, phase, payload, entropy, prev_hash, hash; validates on startup and per spawn/input. ✅ drift.log appends on entropy > 0.4 or χ(t) > 500ms; soft-blocks agent (sets status=DRIFT_BLOCKED). ✅ χ(t) logs exceed events with +0.2 entropy bump, doesn’t crash CLI. ✅ Agents auto-restore on init; agents/<id>.json and memory_stack/<id>_memory.json created/reloadable. ✅ Resonance: Agent.run rejects non-resonant inputs via hash check. ✅ No network/cloud/web. Where: Create README.md in protoforge/. On Linux/macOS: nano README.md, paste, save (Ctrl+O, Enter, Ctrl+X). On Windows: Open Notepad, paste, save as README.md in protoforge/. What/Why: Documents setup, commands, and tests per the ruling. Includes Grok’s expanded test script and acceptance criteria for clarity. The link to Python downloads addresses Grok’s version check guidance. Ready to move on? Confirm README.md is in protoforge/ and readable (cat README.md or open in Notepad). If you need help with file creation, let me know. 3) Create Core Python Files
Goal: Implement the CLI core with full code for all modules, addressing Grok’s fixes for memory_stack/, χ(t), resonance, soft-block, and parsing/logging.

       mem_p = memory_dir / f"{agent_id}_memory.json"
       if mem_p.exists():

Where: Create src/agent.py.

Use nano src/agent.py (Linux/macOS) or Notepad (Windows), paste, save. What/Why: Implements the Agent class with echo/writer types, resonance check in run (per Grok’s fix), and state persistence to memory_stack/ (via restore). state is restricted to last_input, last_output, status. Resonance checks input payload against the last input echo record for the agent.

def write_echo(storage: Path, actor: str, phase: str, payload: dict, entropy: float):
   global prev_hash
   echo_path = _get_echo_file(storage)
   prev_hash = _load_prev_hash(echo_path)

curr_hash = hashlib.sha256((prev_hash + json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True)).encode()).hexdigest()
   rec = {
       "ts": time.time(),
       "actor": actor,
       "phase": phase,
       "payload": payload,
       "entropy": float(max(0.0, min(1.0, entropy))),
       "prev_hash": prev_hash,
       "hash": curr_hash,
   }
   echo_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   with echo_path.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
       f.write(json.dumps(rec) + "\n")
   prev_hash = curr_hash
def write_chi_event(storage: Path, phase: str, exceeded_ms: float, ent_bump: float):
   payload = {"event": "chi_exceeded", "phase": phase, "exceeded_ms": exceeded_ms, "entropy_bump": ent_bump}
   write_echo(storage, "orchestrator", "drift", payload, ent_bump)
Where: Create src/memory/echo.py with nano/Notepad, paste, save.

What/Why: Implements echo logging with hash chaining (sha256(prev_hash + sorted_json_payload)), startup validation, and robust JSON error handling (per Grok’s fix). Single prev_hash avoids multi-path complexity.

Create src/memory/drift.py:
Command: Copy the following into protoforge/src/memory/drift.py:
 python
import json, time, hashlib
from pathlib import Path
from collections import deque
from .echo import write_echo
def _entropy_of_payload(payload: dict, last_hashes: deque) -> float:
   p_str = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True)
   size_factor = len(p_str) / 1000.0
   h = hashlib.sha256(p_str.encode()).hexdigest()
   novelty = 1.0 if h not in last_hashes else 0.0
   ent = min(1.0, size_factor + novelty)
   last_hashes.append(h)
   return ent
class DriftMonitor:
   def __init__(self, echo_path: Path, drift_path: Path, threshold: float = 0.4):
       self.storage = echo_path.parent.parent  # For chi_timer
       self.echo_path = echo_path
       self.drift_path = drift_path
       self.threshold = threshold
       self.last_hashes = deque(maxlen=5)
       if echo_path.exists():
           try:
               lines = echo_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip().splitlines()[-5:]
               for ln in lines:
                   if ln.strip():
                       rec = json.loads(ln)
                       self.last_hashes.append(rec.get("hash", ""))
           except Exception:
               pass
   def _log_drift(self, payload: dict, entropy: float, note: str = ""):
       self.drift_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
       evt = {"ts": time.time(), "entropy": float(entropy), "payload": payload, "note": note}
       with self.drift_path.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
           f.write(json.dumps(evt) + "\n")
   def check_and_firewall(self, payload: dict, agent_id: str | None = None, orchestrator: 'Orchestrator' = None, ent_bump: float = 0.0) -> float:
       ent = _entropy_of_payload(payload, self.last_

ts[agent_id].state["status"] = "DRIFT_BLOCKED"
               orchestrator.agents[agent_id].hibernate(orchestrator.storage / "agents")
       return ent
Where: Create src/memory/drift.py, paste, save.

What/Why: Implements entropy calculation (size + novelty), logs high entropy (>0.4) to drift.log, and soft-blocks agents directly (per Grok’s fix). Integrates with echo logging. Create src/memory/chi_timer.py:
Command: Copy the following into protoforge/src/memory/chi_timer.py:
 python
import time
from contextlib import contextmanager
from .echo import write_chi_event
DEFAULT_MS = 500
ENTROPY_BUMP = 0.2
@contextmanager
def phase_timer(phase: str, drift_monitor, storage: Path, max_ms: int = DEFAULT_MS):
   start = time.time()
   try:
       yield
   finally:
       delta_ms = (time.time() - start) * 1000.0
       if delta_ms > max_ms:
           write_chi_event(storage, phase, delta_ms, ENTROPY_BUMP)
           drift_monitor._log_drift({"event": "chi_exceeded", "phase": phase, "exceeded_ms": delta_ms}, ENTROPY_BUMP, note="chi_exceeded")
Where: Create src/memory/chi_timer.py, paste, save. What/Why: Implements χ(t) timer with 500ms threshold, logs to echo.log and drift.log on exceed, and applies +0.2 entropy bump (per Grok’s fix). Non-blocking as ruled.

Create src/orchestrator.py:
Command: Copy the following into protoforge/src/orchestrator.py:
 python
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict
from .agent import Agent, BLOCKED
from .memory.echo import write_echo, validate_echo_chain
from .memory.drift import DriftMonitor
from .memory.chi_timer import phase_timer
class Orchestrator:
   def __init__(self, storage: Path):
       self.storage = storage
       (self.storage / "agents").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
       (self.storage / "logs").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
       (self.storage / "memory_stack").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
       self.agents: Dict[str, Agent] = {}
       self.drift = DriftMonitor(self.storage / "logs" / "echo.log", self.storage / "logs" / "drift.log")
       validate_echo_chain(self.storage / "logs" / "echo.log")
       self._auto_restore()
   def _auto_restore(self):
       agents_dir = self.storage / "agents"
       for p in agents_dir.glob("*.json"):
           try:
               a = Agent.restore(agents_dir, self.storage / "memory_stack", p.stem)
               self.agents[a.id] = a
           except Exception:
               pass  # Ignore corrupt files for MVP
   def spawn(self, task_type: str, name: str | None = None) -> str:
       validate_echo_chain(self.storage / "logs" / "echo.log")
       with phase_timer("spawn", self.drift, self.storage):
           a = Agent.new(task_type, name)
           self.agents[a.id] = a
           rec = {"cmd": "spawn", "args": {"task_type": task_type, "name": name}, "agent_id": a.id}
           ent = self.drift.check_and_firewall(rec, a.id, self)
           write_echo(self.storage, "orchestrator", "persist", rec, ent)
           return a.id
   def list_agents(self):
       l

st = [{"id": a.id, "task_type": a.task_type, "name": a.name, "status": a.state.get("status", "OK")}
              for a in self.agents.values()]
       ent = self.drift.check_and_firewall({"cmd": "list", "result_count": len(lst)})
       write_echo(self.storage, "cli", "query", {"cmd": "list"}, ent)
       return lst
   def route_input(self, agent_id: str, payload: dict) -> dict:
       validate_echo_chain(self.storage / "logs" / "echo.log")
       with phase_timer("route", self.drift, self.storage):
           if agent_id not in self.agents:
               out = {"error": "unknown agent"}
               ent = self.drift.check_and_firewall({"cmd": "input", "args": {"agent_id": agent_id, "payload": payload}, "error": "unknown"})
               write_echo(self.storage, "orchestrator", "execute", out, ent)
               return out
           a = self.agents[agent_id]
           out = a.run(payload, self.storage)
           ent = self.drift.check_and_firewall({"cmd": "output", "args": out}, agent_id, self)
           write_echo(self.storage, "orchestrator", "execute", {"cmd": "output", "args": out}, ent)
           self.persist_memory(agent_id)
           return out
   def hibernate(self, agent_id: str):
       if agent_id in self.agents:
           self.agents[agent_id].hibernate(self.storage / "agents")
           ent = self.drift.check_and_firewall({"cmd": "hibernate", "agent_id": agent_id})
           write_echo(self.storage, "orchestrator", "persist", {"cmd": "hibernate", "agent_id": agent_id}, ent)
   def restore(self, agent_id: str):
       agents_dir = self.storage / "agents"
       a = Agent.restore(agents_dir, self.storage / "memory_stack", agent_id)
       a.state["status"] = "OK"
       self.agents[a.id] = a

, agent_id: str):
       agents_dir = self.storage / "agents"
       a = Agent.restore(agents_dir, self.storage / "memory_stack", agent_id)
       a.state["status"] = "OK"
       self.agents[a.id] = a

ent = self.drift.check_and_firewall({"cmd": "restore", "agent_id": agent_id})
       write_echo(self.storage, "orchestrator", "persist", {"cmd": "restore", "agent_id": agent_id}, ent)
   def persist_memory(self, agent_id: str):
       a = self.agents[agent_id]
       p = self.storage / "memory_stack" / f"{agent_id}_memory.json"
       p.write_text(json.dumps(a.state, indent=2))
Where: Create src/orchestrator.py, paste, save.

What/Why: Manages agents, auto-restores from agents/, persists state to memory_stack/, and integrates echo/drift/χ(t). Per-action validation and soft-block logic are wired in (per Grok’s fixes).

Create src/main.py:
Command: Copy the following into protoforge/src/main.py:
 python
import json, sys, shlex
from pathlib import Path
from .orchestrator import Orchestrator
from .memory.chi_timer import phase_timer, ENTROPY_BUMP
from .memory.echo import write_echo
assert sys.version_info >= (3, 10), "Python 3.10+ required"
STORAGE = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / "storage"
HELP = """commands:
 spawn <echo|writer> [--name NAME]
 list
 input <agent_id> --json '{...}'
 hibernate <agent_id>
 restore <agent_id>
 logs echo [--tail N]
 logs drift [--tail N]
 help
 exit
"""
def _tail(path: Path, n: int | None):
   if not path.exists():
       print("[]"); return
   lines = []
   with path.open('rb') as f:
       f.seek(0, 2)
       size = f.tell()
       back = min(size, 8192)
       f.seek(-back, 2)
       lines = f.read().decode('utf-8', errors='ignore').strip().splitlines()
   if n is not None:
       lines = lines[-max(0, int(n)):]
   print('\n'.join(lines))
def repl():
   orch = Orchestrator(STORAGE)
   print("ProtoForge CLI — type 'help' for commands.")
   while True:
       try:
           line = input("pf> ").strip()
       except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
           print("\nbye"); break
       if not line:
           continue
       if line in ("exit", "quit"):
           break
       if line == "help":
           print(HELP); continue
       try:
           parts = shlex.split(line)
       except ValueError as e:
           print(f"parse error: {e}")
           continue
       cmd = parts[0]
       if cmd == "spawn":
           if len(parts) < 2:
               print("usage: spawn <echo|writer> [--name NAME]"); continue
           t = parts[1]
           name = None
           if "--name" in parts:

" in parts:
               idx = parts.index("--tail")
               if idx+1 < len(parts): n = int(parts[idx+1])
           with phase_timer("query", orch.drift, STORAGE):
               _tail(p, n)

ent = orch.drift.check_and_firewall({"cmd": "logs", "type": parts[1]})
               write_echo(STORAGE, "cli", "query", {"cmd": "logs", "type": parts[1]}, ent)
       else:
           print("unknown command")
if __name__ == "__main__":
   repl()
Where: Create src/main.py, paste, save.

What/Why: Implements the CLI REPL with shlex parsing, optimized _tail (per Grok’s fix), and consolidated echo writes. All commands integrate χ(t)/drift checks. Ready to move on? Confirm all files (agent.py, orchestrator.py, echo.py, drift.py, chi_timer.py, main.py) are in protoforge/src/ or protoforge/src/memory/. Run ls src/ and ls src/memory/ to verify. If any file creation failed, let me know. 4) Seed echo.log
Goal: Create an initial echo.log entry to show the format and ensure hash chaining starts correctly. Micro-steps:
Run a Python command to seed the log:
Command:
 bash
python -c "import json, hashlib, time; rec = {'ts': time.time(), 'actor': 'system', 'phase': 'persist', 'payload': {'note': 'seed'}, 'entropy': 0.0, 'prev_hash': 'genesis', 'hash': hashlib.sha256(('genesis' + json.dumps({'note': 'seed'}, sort_keys=True)).encode()).hexdigest()}; with open('storage/logs/echo.log', 'w') as f: f.write(json.dumps(rec) + '\n')"
Where: Terminal, inside protoforge/ with venv activated. What/Why: Writes a seed record to storage/logs/echo.log with the correct format (ts, actor, phase, payload, entropy, prev_hash, hash). Ensures the hash chain starts properly. Verify echo.log:
Command: cat storage/logs/echo.log (Linux/macOS) or type storage\logs\echo.log (Windows). Where: Terminal, inside protoforge/. What/Why: Confirms the seed record exists. You should see a JSON line like {"ts": ..., "actor": "system", "phase": "persist", ...}. Ready to move on? Check that storage/logs/echo.log exists and contains a JSON line. If not, rerun the seed command or let me know the error. 5) Run the CLI
Goal: Start the CLI and verify basic functionality. Micro-steps:
Set PYTHONPATH:
Linux/macOS:
Command: export PYTHONPATH=src
Where: Terminal, inside protoforge/.

let me know the error. 5) Run the CLI

What/Why: Verifies new entries for spawn with phase: persist, actor: orchestrator, and chained hashes. Ready to move on? Confirm the CLI starts, help works, and spawn prints a UUID. Check echo.log for new entries. If you hit errors (e.g., import failures), share them, and I’ll debug with you. 6) Run Full Test Script

Start CLI:

Open src/orchestrator.py in nano/Notepad. In route_input, add import time at the top and time.sleep(1) inside the with phase_timer("route", ...): block, before if agent_id not in self.agents. Save the file. Command:

python -m src.main

Where: Terminal and CLI REPL. What/Why: Forces a χ(t) exceed (>500ms), logs to drift.log, and bumps entropy. Check drift.log for chi_exceeded entry. Revert: Remove time.sleep(1) from orchestrator.py after testing. Ready to move on? Confirm all test commands work, drift.log shows entries for large payload/χ(t) exceed, and echo.log has chained records. Share any errors or unexpected outputs. 7) Verify Acceptance Criteria
Goal: Ensure all ruling requirements are met. Micro-steps:
Check commands:
Command: Rerun the test script above. Where: CLI REPL. What/Why: Ensures spawn, input, list, hibernate, restore, logs work without exceptions. Check echo.log:
Command: cat storage/logs/echo.log or type storage\logs\echo.log. Where: Terminal.

What/Why: Verify JSON lines with ts, actor, phase, payload, entropy, prev_hash, hash. Check that hashes chain (each prev_hash matches prior hash). Check drift.log:
Command: cat storage/logs/drift.log or type storage\logs\drift.log. Where: Terminal. What/Why: Confirm entries for entropy >0.4 and χ(t) >500ms, with note: entropy_exceeded or chi_exceeded. Check agent persistence:
Command: ls storage/agents/ or dir storage\agents\. Where: Terminal. What/Why: Verify <agent_id>.json exists after hibernate. Command: ls storage/memory_stack/ or dir storage\memory_stack\. What/Why: Confirm <agent_id>_memory.json exists after input. Check auto-restore:
Command:
 text
python -m src.main
pf> list
pf> exit
Where: CLI REPL. What/Why: Ensures hibernated agents appear in list on restart. Check resonance:
Command: Manually edit storage/logs/echo.log, change a payload field in an input record, then:
 text

def validate_echo_chain(echo_path: Path):
 _ensure_log_dir()
 if not echo_path.exists():
 return
 with open(echo_path, 'r') as f:
 lines = f.readlines()
 if not lines:
 return
 curr_prev = "genesis"
 for line in lines:
 rec = json.loads(line)
 expected_hash = hashlib.sha256((curr_prev + json.dumps(rec['payload'], sort_keys=True)).encode()).hexdigest()
 if rec['hash'] != expected_hash:
 raise ValueError("Echo chain invalid—inspect logs")
 curr_prev = rec['hash']
def write_echo(actor: str, phase: str, payload: dict, entropy: float):
 _ensure_log_dir()
 global prev_hash
 ts = time.time()
 p_str = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True)
 curr_hash = hashlib.sha256((prev_hash + p_str).encode()).hexdigest()
 rec = {
 "ts": ts,
 "actor": actor,
 "phase": phase,
 "payload": payload,
 "entropy": entropy,
 "hash": curr_hash,
 "prev_hash": prev_hash,
 }
 echo_path = Path('storage/logs/echo.log')
 with open(echo_path, 'a') as f:
 f.write(json.dumps(rec) + '\n')
 prev_hash = curr_hash
 """

drift.py
drift_py = """
 import json, hashlib, time
 from collections import deque
 from pathlib import Path
class DriftMonitor:
 def init(self, echo_path: Path):
 self.echo_path = echo_path
 self.last_hashes = deque(maxlen=5)
 if echo_path.exists():
 with open(echo_path, 'r') as f:
 for line in f.readlines()[-5:]:
 if not line.strip():
 continue
 try:
 rec = json.loads(line)
 self.last_hashes.append(rec.get('hash', ''))
 except Exception:
 continue
Always show details
def entropy(self, payload: dict) -> float:
    p_str = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True)
    size_factor = len(p_str) / 1000.0

h = hashlib.sha256(p_str.encode()).hexdigest()
    novelty = 1.0 if h not in self.last_hashes else 0.0
    ent = min(1.0, size_factor + novelty)
    self.last_hashes.append(h)
    return ent
def check_and_firewall(self, payload: dict, agent=None, entropy_extra: float = 0.0) -> float:
    ent = min(1.0, self.entropy(payload) + max(0.0, entropy_extra))
    if ent > 0.4:
        Path('storage/logs').mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        with open('storage/logs/drift.log', 'a') as f:
            f.write(json.dumps({"ts": time.time(), "entropy": ent, "alert": "high_drift", "payload": payload}) + '\\n')
        print(f"WARNING: High drift detected (entropy: {ent:.3f}) — inspect storage/logs/drift.log")
        if agent is not None and hasattr(agent, 'state'):
            agent.state["blocked"] = True
    return ent
"""
 (mem / "drift.py").write_text(drift_py)
chi_timer.py
chi_py = """
 import time, json
 from contextlib import contextmanager
 from pathlib import Path
 from .echo import write_echo
def _log_chi_event(phase: str, exceeded_ms: float):
 payload = {"phase": phase, "exceeded_ms": exceeded_ms, "action": "chi_exceeded"}
 write_echo("system", "drift", payload, entropy=0.0)
 Path('storage/logs').mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 with open('storage/logs/drift.log', 'a') as f:
 f.write(json.dumps({"ts": time.time(), **payload}) + '\n')
@contextmanager
 def phase_timer(phase: str, max_ms: int = 500):
 start = time.time()
 state = {"exceeded": False, "delta_ms": 0.0}
 try:
 yield state
 finally:
 delta = (time.time() - start) * 1000.0
 if delta > max_ms:
 state["exceeded"] = True
 state["delta_ms"] = delta
 _log_chi_event(phase, delta)
 """
 (mem / "chi_timer.py").write_text(chi_py)

def persist_memory(self, agent_id: str):
    if agent_id not in self.agents:
        raise ValueError("Agent not found")
    a = self.agents[agent_id]
    p = self.storage / "memory_stack" / f"{a.id}_memory.json"
    p.write_text(json.dumps(a.state, indent=2))
"""
 (src / "orchestrator.py").write_text(orchestrator_py)

What it does (per the ruling)
Echo log (append-only, JSONL) with phase labels and hash chaining:
 storage/logs/echo.log (already seeded with a first record)

Drift monitor: entropy = size(kB) + novelty(0/1); threshold 0.4
 On high drift: warns + soft-blocks the specific agent (state.blocked = True) and logs to storage/logs/drift.log

d with a first record)

Drift monitor: entropy = size(kB) + novelty(0/1); threshold 0.4
 On high drift: warns + soft-blocks the specific agent (state.blocked = True) and logs to storage/logs/drift.log

χ(t) grace timer: 500ms; logs exceed events to echo + drift; also bumps the next entropy check by +0.2 but never halts the CLI

Agents: echo, writer only; no cloud calls, no dynamic code gen

Auto-restore: any JSON in storage/agents/*.json is reloaded on startup (and unblocked)

Memory: per-agent state persisted to storage/memory_stack/<id>_memory.json after each input

Acceptance checklist (you can verify in seconds)
spawn, input, list, hibernate, restore, logs all work

echo.log shows JSON lines with phase, entropy, hash, prev_hash

drift.log receives entries on high entropy or χ(t) exceed

Agents that drift become DRIFT_BLOCKED until restore <id>

Restarting the CLI shows previously hibernated agents (auto-restore)
